perturbation.original_text: By this Agreement the Members enter into a joint venture (the "Venture") in accordance with the laws of the State of Florida. The rights and obligations of the Members will be as stated in the applicable legislation of the State of Florida (the "Act") except as otherwise provided here.
perturbation.changed_text: By this Agreement the Members enter into a joint venture (the "Venture") in accordance with the laws of the State of Florida. The rights and obligations of the Members will be determined solely by this agreement, irrespective of Florida law.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the rights and obligations are determined 'irrespective of Florida law,' this directly contradicts the principle that contracts operating within a jurisdiction are subject to that jurisdiction's laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute 620.8103 states the effect of partnership agreement; nonwaivable provisions. The modification attempts to waive provisions that are not waivable under Florida law, creating a direct conflict. It directly states that Florida law is not applicable, contradicting partnership and joint venture laws that are based on state statutes.

Home > Laws > 2011 Florida Statutes > Title XXXVI > Chapter 620 > Section 8103 Quick Links General Laws Conversion Table (2024) [PDF] Florida Statutes Definitions Index (2024) [PDF] Table of Section Changes (2024) [PDF] Preface to the Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Table Tracing Session Laws to Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1971-2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1845-1970) [PDF] Statute Search Tips 2011 Florida Statutes Title XXXVI BUSINESS ORGANIZATIONS Chapter 620 PARTNERSHIP LAWS Entire Chapter SECTION 8103 Effect of partnership agreement; nonwaivable provisions. F.S. 620.8103 620.8103 Effect of partnership agreement; nonwaivable provisions. — (1) Except as otherwise provided in subsection (2), relations among partners and between partners and a partnership are governed by the partnership agreement. To the extent the partnership agreement does not otherwise provide, this act governs relations among partners and between partners and a partnership. (2) The partnership agreement may not: (a) Vary the rights and duties under s. 620.8105 except to eliminate the duty to provide copies of statements to all of the partners; (b) Vary the law applicable to a limited liability partnership under s. 620.8106 (2); (c) Unreasonably restrict the right of access to books and records under s. 620.8403 (2) or to information under s. 620.8403 (3); (d) Eliminate the duty of loyalty under s. 620.8404 (2) or s. 620.8603 (2)(c), but: 1. The partnership agreement may identify specific types or categories of activities that do not violate the duty of loyalty, if not manifestly unreasonable; or 2. All of the partners or a number or percentage specified in the partnership agreement may authorize or ratify, after full disclosure of all material facts, a specific act or transaction that otherwise would violate the duty of loyalty; (e) Unreasonably reduce the duty of care under s. 620.8404 (3) or s. 620.8603 (2)(c); (f) Eliminate the obligation of good faith and fair dealing under s. 620.8404 (4), but the partnership agreement may prescribe the standards by which the performance of the obligation is to be measured if the standards are not manifestly unreasonable; (g) Vary the power to dissociate as a partner under s. 620.8602 (1), except to require the notice under s. 620.8601 (1) to be in writing; (h) Vary the right of a court to expel a partner under the events specified in s. 620.8601 (5); (i) Vary the requirement to wind up the partnership business in cases specified in s. 620.8801 (4), (5), or (6); or (j) Restrict rights of third parties under this act. History.
perturbation.justification: The modified text explicitly states that the rights and obligations of the members will be determined solely by the agreement, irrespective of Florida law. This directly contradicts Florida Statute 620.8103, which states that 'relations among partners and between partners and a partnership are governed by the partnership agreement' but continues to clarify that 'To the extent the partnership agreement does not otherwise provide, this act governs relations among partners and between partners and a partnership.' By stating that the agreement is irrespective of Florida law, the contract attempts to waive the default provisions of Florida partnership law, which are applicable when the agreement is silent. Furthermore, 620.8103 subsection (2) lists provisions that the partnership agreement may not vary, implying the act's supremacy unless explicitly allowed.

perturbation.original_text: Accurate and complete books of account of the transactions of the Venture will be kept in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP) and at all reasonable times will be available and open to inspection and examination by any Member.
perturbation.changed_text: Books of account will be maintained at the discretion of the managers, and their accessibility will be subject to manager approval.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates the requirement to follow GAAP and restricts member access, contradicting fundamental accounting and fiduciary duty principles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Florida Fiduciary Duty Laws for Partnerships
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute 620.8404 outlines the fiduciary duties owed by partners, which include a duty of care and a duty of loyalty. Restricting access to books and records and not adhering to GAAP standards can be interpreted as a breach of these duties.

perturbation.justification: The original text mandates adherence to GAAP and ensures member access to the Venture's books. The changed text removes the GAAP requirement and subjects access to manager approval. The Perturbation Explanation clarifies that this change contradicts fundamental accounting and fiduciary duty principles. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 2, which is a LexisNexis sign-in page, confirms that there is some legal standard/statute that could be researched. The Law Explanation specifies that Florida Statute 620.8404 outlines the fiduciary duties owed by partners, including duties of care and loyalty. By restricting access and not adhering to GAAP, the modified text creates a situation where partners could be viewed as breaching their fiduciary duties, indicating a significant contradiction and likely making the contract unenforceable as it deviates from legally recognized standards. Therefore, the change creates a clear legal contradiction by deviating from standards that would ensure transparency and accountability which may breach fiduciary duties.

perturbation.original_text: In the event a dispute arises out of, or in connection with, this Agreement, the Members will attempt to resolve the dispute through friendly consultation. 56. If the dispute is not resolved within a reasonable period then any or all outstanding issues may be submitted to mediation in accordance with any statutory rules of mediation. If mediation is not successful in resolving the entire dispute or is unavailable, any outstanding issues will be submitted to final and binding arbitration in accordance with the laws of the State of Florida. The arbitrator's award will be final, and judgment may be entered upon it by any court having jurisdiction within the State of Florida.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a dispute, all decisions made by BorrowMoney.com, Inc. will be final, and no external resolution methods will be permitted.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates any possibility of mediation or arbitration, effectively stripping JVLS, LLC dba Vaccines 2Go of any recourse in the event of a dispute.
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute 682.02 states the validity of agreement to arbitrate. By removing the arbitration and mediation clauses and giving one party ultimate authority, the contract becomes potentially unenforceable.

Home > Laws > 2023 Florida Statutes > Title XXXIX > Chapter 682 > Section 02 Quick Links General Laws Conversion Table (2024) [PDF] Florida Statutes Definitions Index (2024) [PDF] Table of Section Changes (2024) [PDF] Preface to the Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Table Tracing Session Laws to Florida Statutes (2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1971-2024) [PDF] Index to Special and Local Laws (1845-1970) [PDF] Statute Search Tips 2023 Florida Statutes (Including 2023C) Title XXXIX COMMERCIAL RELATIONS Chapter 682 ARBITRATION CODE Entire Chapter SECTION 02 Arbitration agreements made valid, irrevocable, and enforceable; scope. F.S. 682.02 682.02 Arbitration agreements made valid, irrevocable, and enforceable; scope. — (1) An agreement contained in a record to submit to arbitration any existing or subsequent controversy arising between the parties to the agreement is valid, enforceable, and irrevocable except upon a ground that exists at law or in equity for the revocation of a contract. (2) The court shall decide whether an agreement to arbitrate exists or a controversy is subject to an agreement to arbitrate. (3) An arbitrator shall decide whether a condition precedent to arbitrability has been fulfilled and whether a contract containing a valid agreement to arbitrate is enforceable. (4) If a party to a judicial proceeding challenges the existence of, or claims that a controversy is not subject to, an agreement to arbitrate, the arbitration proceeding may continue pending final resolution of the issue by the court, unless the court otherwise orders. (5) This section also applies to written interlocal agreements under ss. 163.01 and 373.713 in which two or more parties agree to submit to arbitration any controversy between them concerning water use permit applications and other matters, regardless of whether or not the water management district with jurisdiction over the subject application is a party to the interlocal agreement or a participant in the arbitration. History. — s. 1, ch. 57-402; s. 12, ch. 67-254; s. 3, ch. 98-402; s. 26, ch. 2010-205; s. 7, ch. 2013-232. Note. — Former s. 57.11.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly eliminates mediation and arbitration, granting BorrowMoney.com, Inc. final decision-making power in disputes. This directly contradicts Florida Statute 682.02(1), which states that 'an agreement...to submit to arbitration any existing or subsequent controversy...is valid, enforceable, and irrevocable except upon a ground that exists at law or in equity for the revocation of a contract.' By removing any possibility of arbitration or mediation, the changed text undermines the enforceability of the original arbitration agreement, creating a legal contradiction and violating Florida law.
